title: Blanket clippy suppressions at main.rs file scope hide real SRP violations
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Documentation & Intent / YAGNI
Location: src/main.rs:7-24 — #![allow(clippy::too_many_lines, option_if_let_else, or_fun_call, manual_let_else, match_same_arms, items_after_statements, used_underscore_binding, trivially_copy_pass_by_ref, similar_names, cast_precision_loss, map_unwrap_or, literal_string_with_formatting_args, default_trait_access, assigning_clones, collection_is_never_read, format_push_string)]
Violation: File-scope allow() disables 16 pedantic lints. The comment ('large CLI handler functions are inherently long') is a rationalization for the God function documented in #262. Per user's stated policy (no_allow_dead_code: never suppress warnings, fix the root cause), these suppressions are explicitly disallowed. 'too_many_lines' is a SRP detector; silencing it blocks the refactor signal. 'collection_is_never_read' is a dead-code detector — suppressing it hides dead state.
Mandated Refactor: Remove the entire #![allow(...)] block. Address each warning individually: split functions (resolves too_many_lines), fix idioms (option_if_let_else, manual_let_else), rename confusables (similar_names). Pair this issue with #262 (cmd_chat split) — that refactor alone will eliminate half these warnings.
